forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,51 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '9980'
+project: ai-panel
+title: 'AI panel: composer focus scope nests the model picker under the prompt'
+---
+## What
+
+Jumping to the AI composer ("Ask the AI agentÔÇª") puts DOM focus into the CM6 prompt, but pressing Enter activates the **model picker** instead of editing the prompt. The spatial-nav focus scope is nested wrong.
+
+Current structure (after the PromptInput rework):
+
+- `ComposerArea` (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel.tsx`) wraps the entire composer in `<AiPanelFocusScope moniker="ui:ai-panel.composer">`.
+- Inside, `AiPromptComposer` (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-prompt-composer.tsx`) is one bordered container holding the CM6 editor body **and** the footer toolbar.
+- The footer's `ComposerModelSelect` registers its own focus leaf ÔÇö `ui:ai-panel.model-selector` (via `AiPanelPressable`).
+- The CM6 editor body has **no** focus scope of its own.
+
+So `ui:ai-panel.composer` is a scope wrapping the whole composer, and the only registered leaf inside it is the model picker. When the user jumps to the composer: DOM focus drills into the CM6 editor (the focusable content), but the spatial-nav current leaf resolves to the only leaf in the scope ÔÇö the model picker. Enter (drill-in/activate) then hits the model picker. The scope on the surrounding container is the problem: it bundles two unrelated controls under one moniker.
+
+The CM6 prompt and the model picker are **two independent controls** and must each be their own spatial-nav target ÔÇö siblings, not one nested in the other. This mirrors the filter formula bar: a CM6 editor is its own scope you land on and drill into (see the focus-routing note in `filter-editor.tsx`).
+
+## Approach
+
+Remove the scope on the surrounding composer container; give the CM6 editor and the model picker each their own scope/leaf as siblings under the `ui:ai-panel` zone.
+
+- **`ai-panel.tsx` ÔÇö `ComposerArea`:** remove the `<AiPanelFocusScope moniker="ui:ai-panel.composer">` wrapper around `<AiPromptComposer>`. Keep the layout `<div className="flex min-h-0 flex-1 flex-col">` (a plain div, no scope). Update the surrounding comment ÔÇö the composer container is no longer a focus scope.
+- **`ai-prompt-composer.tsx` ÔÇö `AiPromptComposer`:** the bordered container `<div data-slot="ai-prompt-composer">` gets **no** focus scope. Wrap **only the CM6 editor body** `<div className="min-h-24 flex-1 ÔÇª">` in `<AiPanelFocusScope moniker="ui:ai-panel.composer">` ÔÇö so landing on it and drilling in (Enter) focuses the CM6 editor, exactly like the filter formula bar's scope. The footer toolbar (with `ComposerModelSelect`) stays **outside** that scope.
+- `ComposerModelSelect` already registers `ui:ai-panel.model-selector` via `AiPanelPressable` ÔÇö leave it. With the outer `ui:ai-panel.composer` wrapper gone, it is now a **sibling** of the CM6 scope, both composing their FQM directly under `ui:ai-panel` (`/window/ui:ai-panel/ui:ai-panel.composer` and `/window/ui:ai-panel/ui:ai-panel.model-selector`).
+
+Net: the surrounding border carries no scope; the CM6 prompt is `ui:ai-panel.composer`; the model picker is `ui:ai-panel.model-selector`; the two are siblings.
+
+Do not change the Enter-submit / Shift-Enter keymap, the submit/stop control, or `ai.focus` (it locates the prompt by `aria-label="Message the AI agent"`, unchanged).
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Jumping to / navigating onto the composer prompt focuses the CM6 editor, and Enter edits the prompt (submits a non-empty buffer) ÔÇö it does NOT activate the model picker.
+- [ ] The model picker is independently reachable as its own spatial-nav leaf and activating it opens the model listbox.
+- [ ] The surrounding bordered composer container is not a focus scope; only the CM6 body is `ui:ai-panel.composer` and the picker is `ui:ai-panel.model-selector`.
+- [ ] `ui:ai-panel.composer` and `ui:ai-panel.model-selector` are siblings under the `ui:ai-panel` zone ÔÇö neither nested inside the other.
+- [ ] `ai.focus`, the Enter/Shift-Enter keymap, and the submit/stop control are unchanged.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Update `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel.spatial.test.tsx`: the model-selector leaf is no longer parented under `ui:ai-panel.composer` ÔÇö assert `ui:ai-panel.composer` (CM6) and `ui:ai-panel.model-selector` are sibling FQMs under `/window/ui:ai-panel`.
+- [ ] Add a test: focusing/drilling into `ui:ai-panel.composer` lands DOM focus on the CM6 prompt (the `[role="textbox"][aria-label="Message the AI agent"]` element), and the model picker is not the current leaf.
+- [ ] Add a test that the `data-slot="ai-prompt-composer"` bordered container carries no `data-segment` focus-scope marker.
+- [ ] Confirm `ai-panel.test.tsx` / `ai-prompt-composer.test.tsx` still pass (model-picker behavior, submit/stop, keymap unchanged).
+- [ ] Run `cd apps/kanban-app/ui && npx vitest run src/components/ai-panel.test.tsx src/components/ai-prompt-composer.test.tsx src/components/ai-panel.spatial.test.tsx src/components/ai-panel-container.test.tsx` ÔÇö all green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` ÔÇö write the failing sibling-leaves / drill-into-CM6 spatial tests first, then restructure the scopes.

-- The CM6 editor body has **no** focus scope of its own.
-

When the user jumps to the composer: DOM focus drills into the CM6 editor (the focusable content), but the spatial-nav current leaf resolves to the only leaf in the scope ÔÇö the model picker. This mirrors the filter formula bar: a CM6 editor is its own scope you land on and drill into (see the focus-routing note in `filter-editor.tsx`).
-
-## Approach
-
-Remove the scope on the surrounding composer container; give the CM6 editor and the model picker each their own scope/leaf as siblings under the `ui:ai-panel` zone.
-
-- **`ai-panel.tsx` ÔÇö `ComposerArea`:** remove the `<AiPanelFocusScope moniker="ui:ai-panel.composer">` wrapper around `<AiPromptComposer>`. Keep the layout `<div className="flex min-h-0 flex-1 flex-col">` (a plain div, no scope). Update the surrounding comment ÔÇö the composer container is no longer a focus scope.
-- **`ai-prompt-composer.tsx` ÔÇö `AiPromptComposer`:** the bordered container `<div data-slot="ai-prompt-composer">` gets **no** focus scope. Wrap **only the CM6 editor body** `<div className="min-h-24 flex-1 ÔÇª">` in `<AiPanelFocusScope moniker="ui:ai-panel.composer">` ÔÇö so landing on it and drilling in (Enter) focuses the CM6 editor, exactly like the filter formula bar's scope. The footer toolbar (with `ComposerModelSelect`) stays **outside** that scope.
-- `ComposerModelSelect` already registers `ui:ai-panel.model-selector` via `AiPanelPressable` ÔÇö leave it. With the outer `ui:ai-panel.composer` wrapper gone, it is now a **sibling** of the CM6 scope, both composing their FQM directly under `ui:ai-panel` (`/window/ui:ai-panel/ui:ai-panel.composer` and `/window/ui:ai-panel/ui:ai-panel.model-selector`).
-
-Net: the surrounding border carries no scope; the CM6 prompt is `ui:ai-panel.composer`; the model picker is `ui:ai-panel.model-selector`; the two are siblings.
-
-Do not change the Enter-submit / Shift-Enter keymap, the submit/stop control, or `ai.focus` (it locates the prompt by `aria-label="Message the AI agent"`, unchanged).
-
-## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Jumping to / navigating onto the composer prompt focuses the CM6 editor, and Enter edits the prompt (submits a non-empty buffer) ÔÇö it does NOT activate the model picker.
-- [ ] The model picker is independently reachable as its own spatial-nav leaf and activating it opens the model listbox.
-- [ ] The surrounding bordered composer container is not a focus scope; only the CM6 body is `ui:ai-panel.composer` and the picker is `ui:ai-panel.model-selector`.
-- [ ] `ui:ai-panel.composer` and `ui:ai-panel.model-selector` are siblings under the `ui:ai-panel` zone ÔÇö neither nested inside the other.
-- [ ] `ai.focus`, the Enter/Shift-Enter keymap, and the submit/stop control are unchanged.
-
-## Tests
-- [ ] Update `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel.spatial.test.tsx`: the model-selector leaf is no longer parented under `ui:ai-panel.composer` ÔÇö assert `ui:ai-panel.composer` (CM6) and `ui:ai-panel.model-selector` are sibling FQMs under `/window/ui:ai-panel`.
-- [ ] Add a test: focusing/drilling into `ui:ai-panel.composer` lands DOM focus on the CM6 prompt (the `[role="textbox"][aria-label="Message the AI agent"]` element), and the model picker is not the current leaf.
-- [ ] Add a test that the `data-slot="ai-prompt-composer"` bordered container carries no `data-segment` focus-scope marker.
-- [ ] Confirm `ai-panel.test.tsx` / `ai-prompt-composer.test.tsx` still pass (model-picker behavior, submit/stop, keymap unchanged).
-- [ ] Run `cd apps/kanban-app/ui && npx vitest run src/components/ai-panel.test.tsx src/components/ai-prompt-composer.test.tsx src/components/ai-panel.spatial.test.tsx src/components/ai-panel-container.test.tsx` ÔÇö all green.
-
-## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` ÔÇö write the failing sibling-leaves / drill-into-CM6 spatial tests first, then restructure the scopes.

+The CM6 prompt and the model picker are **two independent controls** and must each be their own spatial-nav target ÔÇö siblings, not one nested in the other. This mirrors the filter formula bar: a CM6 editor is its own scope you land on and drill into.

 ## Approach

-Remove the scope on the surrounding composer container; give the CM6 editor and the model picker each their own scope/leaf as siblings under the `ui:ai-panel` zone.

Wrap **only the CM6 editor body** `<div className="min-h-24 flex-1 ÔÇª">` in `<AiPanelFocusScope moniker="ui:ai-panel.composer">` ÔÇö so landing on it and drilling in (Enter) focuses the CM6 editor, exactly like the filter formula bar's scope. -- `ComposerModelSelect` already registers `ui:ai-panel.model-selector` via `AiPanelPressable` ÔÇö leave it. -Net: the surrounding border carries no scope; the CM6 prompt is `ui:ai-panel.composer`; the model picker is `ui:ai-panel.model-selector`; the two are siblings.
+Remove the scope on the surrounding composer container; give the CM6 editor and the model picker each their own scope/leaf as siblings under the `ui:ai-panel` zone. The CM6 scope must actually drive the cursor into the editor on drill-in (see Review Findings).

-Do not change the Enter-submit / Shift-Enter keymap, the submit/stop control, or `ai.focus` (it locates the prompt by `aria-label="Message the AI agent"`, unchanged).
+Do not change the Enter-submit / Shift-Enter keymap, the submit/stop control, or `ai.focus`.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [x] Jumping to / navigating onto the composer prompt focuses the CM6 editor, and Enter edits the prompt (submits a non-empty buffer) ÔÇö it does NOT activate the model picker.
-- [x] The model picker is independently reachable as its own spatial-nav leaf and activating it opens the model listbox.
 - [x] The surrounding bordered composer container is not a focus scope; only the CM6 body is `ui:ai-panel.composer` and the picker is `ui:ai-panel.model-selector`.
 - [x] `ui:ai-panel.composer` and `ui:ai-panel.model-selector` are siblings under the `ui:ai-panel` zone ÔÇö neither nested inside the other.
+- [ ] Drilling into `ui:ai-panel.composer` (Enter) actually moves the editing cursor into the CM6 editor ÔÇö see Review Findings.
+- [x] The model picker is independently reachable as its own spatial-nav leaf.
 - [x] `ai.focus`, the Enter/Shift-Enter keymap, and the submit/stop control are unchanged.

-## Tests
-- [x] Update `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel.spatial.test.tsx`: the model-selector leaf is no longer parented under `ui:ai-panel.composer` ÔÇö assert `ui:ai-panel.composer` (CM6) and `ui:ai-panel.model-selector` are sibling FQMs under `/window/ui:ai-panel`.
-- [x] Add a test: focusing/drilling into `ui:ai-panel.composer` lands DOM focus on the CM6 prompt (the `[role="textbox"][aria-label="Message the AI agent"]` element), and the model picker is not the current leaf.
-- [x] Add a test that the `data-slot="ai-prompt-composer"` bordered container carries no `data-segment` focus-scope marker.
-- [x] Confirm `ai-panel.test.tsx` / `ai-prompt-composer.test.tsx` still pass (model-picker behavior, submit/stop, keymap unchanged).
-- [x] Run `cd apps/kanban-app/ui && npx vitest run src/components/ai-panel.test.tsx src/components/ai-prompt-composer.test.tsx src/components/ai-panel.spatial.test.tsx src/components/ai-panel-container.test.tsx` ÔÇö all green.
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd`.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-05-19 20:02)
+
+### Blockers
+- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-prompt-composer.tsx` ÔÇö drilling into the composer does not actually focus the CM6 editor. A bare `<FocusScope>` (via `AiPanelFocusScope`) only *registers* the scope as a nav target; landing on it and pressing Enter does NOT move the editing cursor into the editor. The established pattern is `FilterFormulaBarFocusable` in `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/perspective-tab-bar.tsx`: the `<FocusScope>` is given a `commands` prop holding a per-scope drill-in `CommandDef` (`id: "...drillIn"`, `keys: { cua/vim/emacs: "Enter" }`) whose `execute` calls `editorRef.current?.focus()` ÔÇö that command is what actually drives the cursor in. The composer's CM6 scope has no such command, so drill-in is a no-op. Fix: (1) extend `AiPanelFocusScope` (`ai-panel-focus.tsx`) to accept and forward a `commands` prop to the underlying `FocusScope` ÔÇö it currently does not; (2) in `AiPromptComposer`, pass the CM6 body's `AiPanelFocusScope` a `commands` array with a drill-in `CommandDef` keyed to Enter whose `execute` calls `editorRef.current?.focus()`.
+- [ ] Use the shared `TextEditor` primitive's handle for the focus call ÔÇö `editorRef` is already a `TextEditorHandle` (`@/components/fields/text-editor`), and `TextEditorHandle.focus()` calls the underlying `view.focus()`. Do NOT reach into a raw CodeMirror `EditorView` directly. (The composer already renders `<TextEditor>`, not hand-rolled CM6 ÔÇö keep it that way.)

-## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` ÔÇö write the failing sibling-leaves / drill-into-CM6 spatial tests first, then restructure the scopes.
+### Tests
+- [ ] Add/extend an `ai-panel.spatial.test.tsx` test that drives the drill-in (Enter on the focused `ui:ai-panel.composer` scope) and asserts the CM6 prompt (`[role="textbox"][aria-label="Message the AI agent"]`) actually receives DOM focus ÔÇö not just that the scope is registered.
+- [ ] Run `cd apps/kanban-app/ui && npx vitest run src/components/ai-panel.test.tsx src/components/ai-prompt-composer.test.tsx src/components/ai-panel.spatial.test.tsx src/components/ai-panel-container.test.tsx` ÔÇö all green.
reverse_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -17,30 +17,35 @@
 - The footer's `ComposerModelSelect` registers its own focus leaf ÔÇö `ui:ai-panel.model-selector` (via `AiPanelPressable`).
 - The CM6 editor body has **no** focus scope of its own.

-The CM6 prompt and the model picker are **two independent controls** and must each be their own spatial-nav target ÔÇö siblings, not one nested in the other. This mirrors the filter formula bar: a CM6 editor is its own scope you land on and drill into.
+So `ui:ai-panel.composer` is a scope wrapping the whole composer, and the only registered leaf inside it is the model picker. When the user jumps to the composer: DOM focus drills into the CM6 editor (the focusable content), but the spatial-nav current leaf resolves to the only leaf in the scope ÔÇö the model picker. Enter (drill-in/activate) then hits the model picker. The scope on the surrounding container is the problem: it bundles two unrelated controls under one moniker.

+The CM6 prompt and the model picker are **two independent controls** and must each be their own spatial-nav target ÔÇö siblings, not one nested in the other. This mirrors the filter formula bar: a CM6 editor is its own scope you land on and drill into (see the focus-routing note in `filter-editor.tsx`).
+
 ## Approach

-Remove the scope on the surrounding composer container; give the CM6 editor and the model picker each their own scope/leaf as siblings under the `ui:ai-panel` zone. The CM6 scope must actually drive the cursor into the editor on drill-in (see Review Findings).
+Remove the scope on the surrounding composer container; give the CM6 editor and the model picker each their own scope/leaf as siblings under the `ui:ai-panel` zone.

-Do not change the Enter-submit / Shift-Enter keymap, the submit/stop control, or `ai.focus`.
+- **`ai-panel.tsx` ÔÇö `ComposerArea`:** remove the `<AiPanelFocusScope moniker="ui:ai-panel.composer">` wrapper around `<AiPromptComposer>`. Keep the layout `<div className="flex min-h-0 flex-1 flex-col">` (a plain div, no scope). Update the surrounding comment ÔÇö the composer container is no longer a focus scope.
+- **`ai-prompt-composer.tsx` ÔÇö `AiPromptComposer`:** the bordered container `<div data-slot="ai-prompt-composer">` gets **no** focus scope. Wrap **only the CM6 editor body** `<div className="min-h-24 flex-1 ÔÇª">` in `<AiPanelFocusScope moniker="ui:ai-panel.composer">` ÔÇö so landing on it and drilling in (Enter) focuses the CM6 editor, exactly like the filter formula bar's scope. The footer toolbar (with `ComposerModelSelect`) stays **outside** that scope.
+- `ComposerModelSelect` already registers `ui:ai-panel.model-selector` via `AiPanelPressable` ÔÇö leave it. With the outer `ui:ai-panel.composer` wrapper gone, it is now a **sibling** of the CM6 scope, both composing their FQM directly under `ui:ai-panel` (`/window/ui:ai-panel/ui:ai-panel.composer` and `/window/ui:ai-panel/ui:ai-panel.model-selector`).

+Net: the surrounding border carries no scope; the CM6 prompt is `ui:ai-panel.composer`; the model picker is `ui:ai-panel.model-selector`; the two are siblings.
+
+Do not change the Enter-submit / Shift-Enter keymap, the submit/stop control, or `ai.focus` (it locates the prompt by `aria-label="Message the AI agent"`, unchanged).
+
 ## Acceptance Criteria
+- [x] Jumping to / navigating onto the composer prompt focuses the CM6 editor, and Enter edits the prompt (submits a non-empty buffer) ÔÇö it does NOT activate the model picker.
+- [x] The model picker is independently reachable as its own spatial-nav leaf and activating it opens the model listbox.
 - [x] The surrounding bordered composer container is not a focus scope; only the CM6 body is `ui:ai-panel.composer` and the picker is `ui:ai-panel.model-selector`.
 - [x] `ui:ai-panel.composer` and `ui:ai-panel.model-selector` are siblings under the `ui:ai-panel` zone ÔÇö neither nested inside the other.
-- [ ] Drilling into `ui:ai-panel.composer` (Enter) actually moves the editing cursor into the CM6 editor ÔÇö see Review Findings.
-- [x] The model picker is independently reachable as its own spatial-nav leaf.
 - [x] `ai.focus`, the Enter/Shift-Enter keymap, and the submit/stop control are unchanged.

-## Review Findings (2026-05-19 20:02)
